scenario: Right rotator cuff tear — TAC claim, suitable employment

guidedAnswers.clinicalNarrative: Right rotator cuff tear (supraspinatus) sustained in MVA. Patient has suitable capacity for sedentary or light duties with right arm restrictions — no overhead reaching, no lifting greater than 2kg with right arm. Attending physiotherapy and under orthopaedic review for possible surgical repair.

clinicalNarrative: James Kowalski sustained a right rotator cuff tear (supraspinatus partial thickness) in a motor vehicle accident on 10 January 2026. He has suitable capacity for sedentary and light duties with restrictions: no overhead reaching, no lifting greater than 2kg with the right arm. He commenced light duties on 1 February 2026. He is attending physiotherapy three times weekly and is under orthopaedic review. Expected return to full pre-injury duties by 10 April 2026.
